cities.name: Istanbul
cities.tagline: Where East meets West — and every neighborhood feels like a different city.
cities.summary: Istanbul is enormous. Picking the wrong neighborhood can mean a 45-minute commute to everything you came to see. Stay in Sultanahmet if it's your first visit. Stay in Beyoğlu if you want nightlife and rooftops. Cross to Kadıköy if you want to eat like a local.

areas.name: Sultanahmet
areas.oneLiner: The historic peninsula. Walk to Hagia Sophia, the Blue Mosque, and Topkapı in minutes.
pros: Walking distance to every major Old City sight
Tram connects you to the rest of the city
Plenty of boutique hotels in Ottoman mansions
cons: Evenings feel dead — nightlife is across the bridge
areas.verdict: Pick this if it's your first visit and you want to walk to every icon.

areas.name: Beyoğlu (Galata / Karaköy / Cihangir)
areas.oneLiner: The creative heart of Istanbul — rooftop bars, design hotels, street food, Galata Tower views.
pros: Best restaurant and bar scene in the city
Galata Bridge walk to Old Town in 20 minutes
Full of boutique and design hotels
cons: Further from Hagia Sophia (15–20 min tram/walk)
areas.verdict: Pick this for nightlife, food, and design hotels — best second-visit base.

areas.name: Beşiktaş & Ortaköy
areas.oneLiner: Bosphorus-front living — Dolmabahçe Palace, ferries to Asia, waterside cafés under the bridge.
pros: Quick ferry to Kadıköy for dinner
cons: Needs tram/taxi to Old Town (~20 min)
Restaurants near the water are pricier
areas.verdict: Pick this for a Bosphorus-front address with palace-hotel access.

areas.name: Kadıköy (Asian side)
areas.oneLiner: The local side — food halls, indie bars, no tour buses. Best for travelers who've been before.
pros: Moda seaside walk is one of Istanbul's best
cons: Ferry required to reach the Old City
areas.verdict: Pick this for the local feel and lower prices — repeat visitors only.

areas.name: Şişli / Nişantaşı
areas.oneLiner: Istanbul's shopping district — designer boutiques, international chains, business hotels.
pros: Great for shopping and fine dining
cons: Can feel like any global capital
areas.verdict: Pick this for shopping and modern comfort over historic atmosphere.

hotels.name: Four Seasons Hotel Istanbul at Sultanahmet
hotels.whyStay: Former Ottoman prison turned luxury icon, steps from Hagia Sophia. Central courtyard, legendary service.

hotels.name: Sirkeci Mansion
hotels.whyStay: Boutique Ottoman-style hotel 5 min from the Blue Mosque. Rooftop breakfast with Topkapı views.

hotels.name: Cheers Hostel
hotels.whyStay: Clean, social hostel with one of the best hostel rooftop bars in the city, right by Hagia Sophia.

hotels.name: Hotel Amira Istanbul
hotels.whyStay: Consistently top-rated mid-range. Warm staff, rooftop terrace, 3 min walk to the Blue Mosque.

hotels.name: Soho House Istanbul
hotels.whyStay: Restored 19th-century palazzo with rooftop pool, members' club atmosphere, best rooftop in the city.

hotels.name: Georges Hotel Galata
hotels.whyStay: Intimate boutique hotel with Galata Tower views, elegant rooms, rooftop restaurant.

hotels.name: Vault Karakoy, The House Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Early-1900s bank building turned design hotel. Soaring lobby, walking distance to Galata Bridge.

hotels.name: 10 Karakoy Istanbul
hotels.whyStay: Modern art-filled hotel in the gallery district, top-floor restaurant overlooking the Bosphorus.

hotels.name: Marmara Pera
hotels.whyStay: Rooftop infinity pool with panoramic Old Town view. Classic Beyoğlu address on Istiklal.

hotels.name: Çırağan Palace Kempinski
hotels.whyStay: The only true palace hotel on the Bosphorus. Waterfront infinity pool, Ottoman suites, showstopper property.

hotels.name: Shangri-La Bosphorus
hotels.whyStay: Large Bosphorus-view rooms, superb service, excellent Asian restaurants. Near Dolmabahçe Palace.

hotels.name: The Stay Bosphorus
hotels.whyStay: Sleek design hotel right on the water in Ortaköy, under the bridge, with a rooftop restaurant.

hotels.name: Hush Moda Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Boutique property in Istanbul's coolest non-tourist district. Walking distance to seafood restaurants and the Moda seaside.

hotels.name: DoubleTree by Hilton Moda
hotels.whyStay: Reliable brand with views of the Marmara. Ferries to the European side 10 min away.

hotels.name: Wyndham Grand Kalamış Marina
hotels.whyStay: Marina setting, larger rooms, pool — good value for families on the Asian side.

hotels.name: The St. Regis Istanbul
hotels.whyStay: In the heart of Nişantaşı designer district. Impeccable service, great spa, excellent butler service.

hotels.name: Raffles Istanbul
hotels.whyStay: Connected to Zorlu Center mall, with one of the best hotel pools in Istanbul. Family suites available.

hotels.name: Hilton Istanbul Bomonti
hotels.whyStay: Tall tower with panoramic views, big pool, conference-friendly, near the metro.

faqs.q: What's the safest area to stay in Istanbul?
faqs.a: Sultanahmet, Beyoğlu (Galata/Cihangir), Beşiktaş, and Kadıköy are all safe and well-touristed. Istanbul is generally safe; just watch for pickpockets near major sights and avoid poorly lit side streets at night.

faqs.q: How many nights do I need in Istanbul?
faqs.a: Four nights is the sweet spot for first-timers. Three is rushed. Five to six nights let you do the European side properly and still cross to Kadıköy for a day.

Sultanahmet for first-timers who want to walk everywhere historic. Beyoğlu for repeat visitors, food/nightlife lovers, and anyone who wants the 'real Istanbul' energy. Many travelers split: two nights each.

cities.name: Cappadocia
cities.tagline: Cave hotels, fairy chimneys, and the best sunrise balloon ride on Earth.
cities.summary: Cappadocia isn't a city — it's a region of small towns. Göreme is the tourist center and best for first-timers. Uçhisar is quieter and more upscale. Ürgüp is a proper town with more dining. Avanos is for travelers who want to feel removed.

areas.name: Göreme
areas.oneLiner: Ground zero for Cappadocia tourism. Best views of the balloons and easiest access to everything.
pros: Best rooftop positions for balloon sunrise
Walking distance to Open Air Museum
Plenty of restaurants, bars, and tour operators
cons: Can be noisy in peak season
areas.verdict: Pick this if balloon photos at sunrise are non-negotiable.

areas.name: Uçhisar
areas.oneLiner: Quieter, more upscale, and perched high — the best panoramic terraces in Cappadocia.
pros: Home to Cappadocia's most famous luxury cave hotels
cons: Need taxi to reach Göreme restaurants
areas.verdict: Pick this for the highest, quietest, most luxurious cave hotels.

areas.name: Ürgüp
areas.oneLiner: A proper Turkish town — wineries, restaurants, and upscale cave hotels without the tourist-trap feel.
pros: Great cave hotels with a more local feel
cons: Balloons are visible but not directly overhead
areas.verdict: Pick this for the best dining + a less touristy cave-hotel experience.

areas.name: Avanos
areas.oneLiner: Pottery town on the Red River — for travelers who want to feel completely away from the crowds.
cons: Balloons don't usually fly directly here
Taxi required to reach Göreme sights
areas.verdict: Pick this for the quietest base — 15 minutes from the action.

hotels.name: Museum Hotel
hotels.whyStay: The original luxury cave hotel — Relais & Châteaux. Antique-filled suites, heated outdoor pool, jaw-dropping views. Reserve 3+ months ahead.

hotels.name: Argos in Cappadocia
hotels.whyStay: Restored village on the hillside. Underground wine cellar, terrace dinners, exceptional design. Perfect for couples.

hotels.name: Sacred House
hotels.whyStay: Wildly designed cave suites with copper tubs and eclectic art. Unlike any hotel you've stayed in.

hotels.name: Kayakapi Premium Caves
hotels.whyStay: Restored 300-year-old cave dwellings, each suite unique. Jacuzzis, fireplaces, excellent restaurant.

hotels.name: Sultan Cave Suites
hotels.whyStay: The famous carpet-and-balloon-filled terrace you've seen on Instagram. Exceptional balloon-sunrise photos.

hotels.name: Mithra Cave Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Consistently top-rated mid-range cave hotel. Great terrace, friendly staff, strong breakfast.

hotels.name: Taskonaklar
hotels.whyStay: Historic Uçhisar cave residence with huge outdoor terraces overlooking Pigeon Valley.

hotels.name: Koza Cave Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Family-run eco-conscious cave hotel in Göreme with excellent breakfast and strong views.

hotels.name: Artemis Cave Suites
hotels.whyStay: Affordable cave rooms in the heart of Göreme with a terrace good for balloon watching.

hotels.name: Sos Cave Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Simple, clean cave hotel in quieter Avanos, family-run, great value.

faqs.q: How many nights do I need in Cappadocia?
faqs.a: Two full nights minimum — you need two shots at the balloon ride because weather cancels flights often. Three nights lets you also do an ATV sunset tour, the Open Air Museum, and the underground cities without rushing.

faqs.q: Which town is best for the balloon sunrise view?
faqs.a: Uçhisar has the highest terraces. Göreme has the most rooftops directly under where balloons fly. Both are excellent — Uçhisar for panoramic shots, Göreme for up-close photos.

faqs.q: Is a cave hotel worth it?
faqs.a: Yes — it's the whole point of coming. Rooms stay cool in summer, warm in winter, and waking up inside rock is memorable. Pick one with a good terrace.

cities.name: Antalya
cities.tagline: Turkey's Mediterranean capital — beaches, Roman ruins, and resort comfort.
cities.summary: Antalya province is huge. Kaleiçi (the old town) is the charming base for culture travelers. Lara has the mega all-inclusive resorts and the sandy beach. Konyaaltı has a long pebble beach closer to the old town. Choose based on whether you want a beach vacation or a walkable historic base.

areas.name: Kaleiçi (Old Town)
areas.oneLiner: Marina, Ottoman houses, Roman walls — the most atmospheric base for non-beach-only travelers.
pros: Best restaurants and bars in central Antalya
Lots of boutique Ottoman mansion hotels
cons: Not right on the main beach
areas.verdict: Pick this for old-town charm without committing to a big resort.

areas.name: Lara
areas.oneLiner: The big sandy beach strip — home of Turkey's famous themed mega-resorts.
pros: Long sandy beach — rare in Turkey
cons: Mostly inside the resort — limited local flavor
areas.verdict: Pick this for sandy beach + classic all-inclusive resort experience.

areas.name: Konyaaltı
areas.oneLiner: The long pebble beach on the west side — easier walk/tram to the old town.
pros: Mix of resort and city hotels
areas.verdict: Pick this for beach access with easy tram to the old town.

areas.name: Belek
areas.oneLiner: Turkey's golf capital and home to its most lavish 5-star all-inclusives.
cons: Entire experience is inside the resort
areas.verdict: Pick this for golf + ultra-luxury all-inclusive — Turkey's premium tier.

hotels.name: Tuvana Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Restored Ottoman mansions with a courtyard pool in the heart of Kaleiçi. Consistently the top-rated old-town hotel.

hotels.name: Mediterra Art Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beautifully restored 200-year-old mansion turned boutique, in a quiet Kaleiçi lane.

hotels.name: Hotel Alp Paşa
hotels.whyStay: Ottoman-style boutique with a pool, romantic setting, walk to the harbor.

hotels.name: White Garden Pansiyon
hotels.whyStay: Charming, affordable guesthouse in a historic building with a pretty garden courtyard.

hotels.name: Titanic Mardan Palace
hotels.whyStay: Famous themed mega-resort with gondola canals and a huge waterpark. Unreal for kids.

hotels.name: Delphin Imperial
hotels.whyStay: Top-tier Lara all-inclusive with excellent food, great pools, and direct beach access.

hotels.name: Akra Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Modern 5-star with panoramic Mediterranean views, infinity pool, and a short tram ride to Kaleiçi.

hotels.name: Barut Akra Park
hotels.whyStay: Family-friendly all-inclusive on Konyaaltı with kids' programs and close access to town.

hotels.name: Regnum Carya
hotels.whyStay: One of Turkey's most acclaimed all-inclusive resorts — golf course, 12 restaurants, beach club. Multi-generational favorite.

hotels.name: Maxx Royal Belek
hotels.whyStay: Ultra-luxury all-inclusive with a huge private beach, multiple pools, and standout kids' club.

hotels.name: Rixos Premium Belek
hotels.whyStay: Large resort with waterpark and multiple à la carte restaurants — classic Belek experience.

faqs.q: Kaleiçi or the beach resorts — which is better?
faqs.a: Kaleiçi if you want to explore and walk. Lara or Belek if you want to never leave the pool. Many travelers split: 2 nights Kaleiçi, 3 nights at a resort.

faqs.q: What's the best beach in Antalya?
faqs.a: Lara for soft sand and resort vibes. Konyaaltı for the longer promenade and easier tram access to the old town. Both are excellent — pick by beach type.
Yes — it's one of Turkey's most family-friendly destinations. Resorts in Belek and Lara are purpose-built for families with kids.

cities.name: Bodrum
cities.tagline: The Turkish Riviera — chic beach clubs, whitewashed villas, yacht-filled bays.
cities.summary: Bodrum is a peninsula, not just a town. Bodrum Town itself is the nightlife hub. Bitez is family-friendly. Yalıkavak has the luxury marina. Türkbükü is the 'St. Tropez of Turkey' for the quiet-money crowd.

areas.name: Bodrum Town
areas.oneLiner: The lively center with the castle, the marina, and most of the nightlife.
cons: No great beach in town itself
areas.verdict: Pick this for nightlife and a walkable center — the social side.

areas.name: Yalıkavak
areas.oneLiner: Home of the superyacht marina and Bodrum's most stylish boutiques.
areas.verdict: Pick this for the superyacht marina and chic boutique scene.

areas.name: Bitez
areas.oneLiner: The family-friendly bay — shallow water, calmer atmosphere, good value.
areas.verdict: Pick this for the best family-friendly bay on the peninsula.

areas.name: Türkbükü & Göltürkbükü
areas.oneLiner: The discreet 'St. Tropez' — wooden piers, beach clubs, quiet luxury.
pros: Quieter than the peninsula's party zones
cons: No sandy beach — platforms on the sea
areas.verdict: Pick this if money is no object — Turkey's discreet St-Tropez.

hotels.name: Mandarin Oriental Bodrum
hotels.whyStay: Sprawling cliffside resort with multiple private beaches, world-class spa, Nobu restaurant.

hotels.name: Maça Kızı
hotels.whyStay: Iconic Bodrum boutique — whitewashed walls, famous beach club, the in-crowd hotel.

hotels.name: LUX* Bodrum
hotels.whyStay: Large design-forward resort with excellent kids' club, two private beaches, great all-inclusive option.

hotels.name: Caresse, a Luxury Collection Resort
hotels.whyStay: Elegant cliffside 5-star with private beach and infinity pools near Yalıkavak marina.

hotels.name: Kaya Palazzo Resort
hotels.whyStay: All-inclusive resort with excellent food and kids' programs on a private beach.

hotels.name: Su Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Charming adults-only boutique in the heart of Bodrum Town with a courtyard pool.

hotels.name: Marina Vista Bodrum
hotels.whyStay: Great central location right by the marina with a rooftop pool overlooking the castle.

hotels.name: Bitez Garden Life Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Relaxed garden hotel with a pool, 5-min walk to Bitez beach, consistently great reviews.

hotels.name: Jasmin Beach Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Simple beachfront hotel in Bitez with good value and direct bay access.

faqs.q: Which side of Bodrum is best?
faqs.a: North side (Yalıkavak, Türkbükü, Gündoğan) for luxury and views. South side (Bodrum town, Bitez, Gümbet) for nightlife and budget. Cross-peninsula drives are 20–40 minutes.
Parts of it are now among the priciest destinations in the Mediterranean. Bitez and Gümüşlük are still reasonable. Yalıkavak and Türkbükü are genuinely expensive in summer.

cities.name: Fethiye
cities.tagline: Turquoise lagoons, paraglider sunsets, Blue Voyage gulets.
cities.summary: Fethiye itself is a working town; most travelers stay nearby. Ölüdeniz has the famous Blue Lagoon and paragliding. Çalış has a long beach and good value. Kayaköy is the ghost village escape. Hisarönü is the nightlife strip.

areas.name: Ölüdeniz
areas.oneLiner: The famous Blue Lagoon — calm turquoise water and paragliders descending from Babadağ mountain.
pros: Walking distance to Blue Lagoon beach
areas.verdict: Pick this for the iconic Blue Lagoon and paragliding access.

areas.name: Çalış Beach
areas.oneLiner: Long pebble beach with a sunset promenade — better value than Ölüdeniz.
areas.verdict: Pick this for sunsets and better value than Ölüdeniz next door.

areas.name: Kayaköy & Hisarönü
areas.oneLiner: Hisarönü is party central. Kayaköy next door is the peaceful ghost village with design stays.
pros: Gateway to the Lycian Way trail
areas.verdict: Pick this for design hotels (Kayaköy) or budget nightlife (Hisarönü).

hotels.name: Hillside Beach Club
hotels.whyStay: Consistently ranked one of Turkey's best all-inclusive resorts. Private cove, excellent food, adults-only and family pools.

hotels.name: Liberty Hotels Lykia
hotels.whyStay: Large resort in Ölüdeniz with adults-only zone, great kids' club, and direct beach access.

hotels.name: Oyster Residences
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only boutique steps from the Blue Lagoon beach. Romantic and intimate.

hotels.name: Montana Pine Resort
hotels.whyStay: Pine forest resort with multiple pools, kids' programs, dolmuş to Ölüdeniz.

hotels.name: Villa Rhapsody
hotels.whyStay: Peaceful family-run pension in Kayaköy with a pool and the best views of the ghost village.

hotels.name: Yacht Classic Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Design-led hotel on the Fethiye harbor, adults-only, walking distance to the old town.

hotels.name: Jiva Beach Resort
hotels.whyStay: Affordable all-inclusive on Çalış Beach with big pools and family rooms.

faqs.a: Ölüdeniz for the iconic lagoon photo and paragliding. Çalış for better value, quieter vibe, and sunsets. A combined trip (2+2) works well.
Yes — Hillside Beach Club and Liberty Lykia are some of the best family resorts in Turkey. The area is very safe and oriented around beach activities.

cities.name: Izmir
cities.tagline: Turkey's most modern, secular big city — and the gateway to Ephesus.
cities.summary: Izmir is a big urban city that most travelers use as a base to visit Ephesus (Selçuk) or the coast (Çeşme). Alsancak is the walkable foodie center. Konak has the waterfront and Kemeraltı bazaar. For beach and day trips, Çeşme is 1 hour away.

areas.name: Alsancak
areas.oneLiner: The heart of Izmir's restaurant, bar, and boutique scene — walkable and lively.

areas.name: Konak
areas.oneLiner: Waterfront Izmir — clock tower, ferry terminal, and the historic Kemeraltı bazaar.

areas.name: Çeşme
areas.oneLiner: Coastal resort town 1 hour from Izmir — windsurfing, thermal springs, long beaches.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Büyük Efes Izmir
hotels.whyStay: Classic Izmir 5-star with landscaped gardens, big pool, and a central Kordon-adjacent location.

hotels.name: Mövenpick Hotel Izmir
hotels.whyStay: Modern tower hotel with sea views and a rooftop pool, walk to Alsancak restaurants.

hotels.name: Key Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Design hotel in a converted 1950s bank building with rooftop restaurant and waterfront views.

hotels.name: Wyndham Grand Izmir Özdilek
hotels.whyStay: Connected to a mall with pool and family rooms — reliable option for families.

hotels.name: Nomads Hotel Izmir
hotels.whyStay: Affordable central hotel with clean rooms and strong Wi-Fi, steps from Alsancak cafes.

hotels.name: Alavya Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Whitewashed design boutique in Alaçatı village with courtyard pool and top restaurant.

hotels.name: Sheraton Çeşme
hotels.whyStay: Large beach resort with thermal spa and excellent family facilities on the Çeşme coast.

faqs.a: Yes — it's Turkey's most liberal, Aegean, and food-forward big city. Most travelers pair it with Ephesus (1 hour south) or Çeşme (1 hour west).
Selçuk is the small town 5 min from the ruins — best for a quick overnight. Izmir Alsancak is better if you want city amenities and just day-trip to Ephesus.

cities.name: Pamukkale
cities.tagline: The white travertine terraces and the ancient ruins of Hierapolis above them.
cities.summary: Pamukkale is a 1-night stop for most travelers. The village at the base has simple pensions. Karahayıt, 5 km away, has slightly larger thermal-spa hotels. Denizli city is 20 km away if you want a proper city base.

areas.name: Pamukkale Village
areas.oneLiner: Walk right up to the travertines from your hotel — the most convenient base.

areas.name: Karahayıt
areas.oneLiner: Red-water thermal spring village 5 km away, with bigger thermal-spa hotels.

hotels.name: Doga Thermal Health & Spa
hotels.whyStay: Large thermal spa hotel with multiple hot pools, near Pamukkale travertines.

hotels.name: Hierapark Thermal & Spa Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Thermal pools, all-inclusive meal plan, reliable for a spa night.

hotels.name: Hotel Hal-Tur
hotels.whyStay: Well-reviewed mid-range right in Pamukkale village with a pool and easy walk to the travertines.

hotels.name: Venus Suite Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Clean, well-run guesthouse with a pool — excellent value for the area.

hotels.name: Melrose House Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Charming small hotel with a garden pool, consistently top-rated for hospitality.

faqs.q: Do I need to stay overnight in Pamukkale?
faqs.a: Yes if you want a quiet morning or sunset at the travertines. Day-trippers arrive at peak hours and it gets crowded. A cheap village hotel lets you walk over at dawn.

cities.name: Marmaris
cities.tagline: Pine-covered bays, water sports, and Turkey's busiest gulet marina.
cities.summary: Marmaris is a holiday town built for beach-and-boat travelers. The town marina is the nightlife hub. Içmeler is the quieter family-friendly bay. Turunç is the secluded cove 40 minutes away.

areas.name: Marmaris Center
areas.oneLiner: The marina, the bar street, the long town beach — busy and budget-friendly.
cons: Bar Street is loud into the night

areas.name: İçmeler
areas.oneLiner: The calmer bay next door — cleaner water, family-oriented, nicer beach.

hotels.name: Grand Yazıcı Marmaris Palace
hotels.whyStay: Large all-inclusive with kids' club and private beach on Pasha Bay.

hotels.name: D-Resort Grand Azur
hotels.whyStay: Beachfront resort with multiple pools, spa, and adults-only areas.

hotels.name: Casa De Maris Spa & Resort Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only all-inclusive in İçmeler with good food and a quiet bay.

hotels.name: Marti Resort de Luxe
hotels.whyStay: Established İçmeler family resort with beach, water sports, and kids' programs.

hotels.name: Sonnen Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Well-run mid-range near Icmeler Beach with a pool, solid value.

faqs.a: Marmaris if you want nightlife and a buzzing town. İçmeler for families and couples who want a calmer beach.

cities.name: Kaş
cities.tagline: The bohemian Lycian coast town — diving, paragliding, and cobblestone streets.
cities.summary: Kaş is small and charming — a former Greek village on the Mediterranean with no package resorts. Stay in Kaş Center to walk everywhere. Çukurbağ Peninsula (just west) has the boutique sea-view hotels.

areas.name: Kaş Center
areas.oneLiner: Cobblestone old town with cafés, boutique shops, and the harbor — walk everywhere.
pros: Excellent dining for a town this size

areas.name: Çukurbağ Peninsula
areas.oneLiner: The sea-view peninsula with boutique design hotels and private swimming platforms.
pros: Best sea views in the area

hotels.name: Hotel Villa Hera
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only cliffside hotel with panoramic sea views and infinity pool.

hotels.name: Peninsula Gardens
hotels.whyStay: Adults-only sea-view hotel with swimming platforms on the Çukurbağ peninsula.

hotels.name: Gardenia Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beautifully located adults-only boutique in Kaş old town with a rooftop terrace.

hotels.name: Hideaway Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Consistently top-rated friendly small hotel a short walk from the harbor.

hotels.name: Ani Pension
hotels.whyStay: Popular backpacker-friendly pension with a rooftop and central location.

faqs.a: Kaş is quieter and more adults-oriented than Antalya's resort areas. Many hotels are adults-only. Families usually prefer Antalya or Fethiye instead.

cities.name: Trabzon
cities.tagline: The Black Sea gateway — Sumela Monastery, highland plateaus, rainforest green.
cities.summary: Trabzon is the entry point for Turkey's rainy green Black Sea region. Stay in Trabzon Center for the city and Sumela. For the famous Uzungöl lake, base there directly — it's 1.5 hours from the city.

areas.name: Trabzon City Center
areas.oneLiner: The urban base for visiting Sumela Monastery and exploring the Black Sea coast.

areas.name: Uzungöl
areas.oneLiner: The famous mountain lake village — cloud-covered mosques, trout, green everywhere.
pros: Many hotels right on the lake

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel Trabzon
hotels.whyStay: Modern harbor-view hotel with pool and spa, walk to Meydan square.

hotels.name: Novotel Trabzon
hotels.whyStay: Reliable brand with good breakfast, sea views, and central location.

hotels.name: Ramada Hotel Trabzon
hotels.whyStay: Central city hotel with pool and family rooms.

hotels.name: İnan Kardeşler Bungalow
hotels.whyStay: Wood-clad bungalows with balconies over the lake — the classic Uzungöl stay.

hotels.name: Uzungöl Suites Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Central lakeside hotel in Uzungöl village with traditional Black Sea architecture.

faqs.q: When is Trabzon best to visit?
faqs.a: June to September. The Black Sea region is very rainy — pack layers and waterproofs even in summer.
